stories.headline: What we know about the ceasefire between Lebanon and Israel
stories.summary: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: Celebrations in Lebanon as 10-day ceasefire with Israel begins
stories.summary: Celebratory gunfire heard in Beirut as truce takes effect, though Lebanese military reports violations.

stories.headline: ‘Trump forced Israel into a ceasefire’ with Lebanon

stories.headline: Iran war live: Ceasefire starts in Lebanon as Trump says Tehran deal close

stories.headline: US-brokered 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon goes into effect
stories.summary: Trump extended an invitation to Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to hold the first direct meeting at that level in decades.

stories.headline: Australia's most decorated living soldier granted bail over war crime charges
stories.summary: Lawyers argued that Ben Roberts-Smith wouldn't be able to defend himself properly from prison.

stories.summary: Former soldier is accused of murdering five people in Afghanistan.

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan meet Bondi shooting survivors
stories.summary: On the fourth day of their Australian visit, the royal couple pay tribute to the 15 people killed in the Bondi shooting.

stories.headline: Kenya Seeks Emergency World Bank Funds to Absorb Iran War Impact
stories.summary: Kenya is in talks with the World Bank for emergency funding to help deal with the repercussions of the Iran war, according to central bank Governor Kamau Thugge.

stories.headline: Trump calls war on Iran a ‘little diversion’
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has described the war on Iran which has killed more than 2,000 people as a ‘little diversion’.

stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: London Metals Index at Record High on Aluminum ‘Black Hole’ Fear
stories.summary: Industrial metals jumped to a record high on the London Metal Exchange, driven by gains in aluminum after the Middle East war disrupted supplies, as well as a recent revival in copper.

stories.headline: China Moves to Slow Yuan Rally by Cooling Fixing Volatility
stories.summary: China’s central bank is using its daily reference rate to temper the yuan’s rally as the currency’s outperformance during the Iran war fuels further bullish sentiment.

stories.headline: China to Commission Seven Nuclear Reactors in 2026, CCTV Says
stories.summary: Seven nuclear power reactors are scheduled to be completed and commissioned in China this year, state broadcaster China Central Television reported, citing the nation’s atomic energy organization’s annual report.

stories.headline: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
stories.summary: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.

stories.headline: Virginia Ex-Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax Kills Wife and Self, Police Say
stories.summary: Mr. Fairfax served as lieutenant governor from 2018 to 2022. The couple’s teenage children were home, the police said, and one of them called 911.

stories.headline: Ukraine war briefing: €90bn EU loan for Ukraine to be released in second quarter
stories.summary: EU economy commissioner says Iran war is feeding Russia’s war machine; Trump condemns massive strikes on Ukraine. What we know on day 1,513The EU expects to start releasing a new €90bn loan to Ukraine in the second quarter, the bloc’s economy chief told AFP on Thursday. stories.headline: Trump’s ‘fossil fuelled war’ is the perfect opportunity to end reliance on oil and gas, experts say
stories.summary: Experts are calling on governments to ‘reduce the risk of every fossil fuel disruption turning into a global energy and economic shock’.

stories.headline: Artemis commander tells BBC about 'powerful' moment crater named after his late wife
stories.summary: Reid Wiseman’s two daughters were in Nasa’s mission control room for the naming of the “Carroll” crater in honour of the commander’s late wife.

stories.headline: Major refinery fire won't lead to fuel rationing, Australian PM says
stories.summary: A 13-hour blaze at one of Australia's two oil refineries could impact petrol prices and supply.

stories.headline: Welcome to the age of hoarding

stories.headline: Global Banks Scramble for Land in Mumbai
stories.summary:
Mumbai’s skyline is changing as Wall Street firms pile in to tap India’s financial boom. But in a land-starved city, that growth is colliding with communities and intensifying the fight for space.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Oil Shock May Weigh on Equities: Morgan Stanley’s Sibal
stories.summary: Morgan Stanley analyst Rajeev Sibal tells Bloomberg TV that global equity markets have not fully absorbed the impact of the energy shock stemming from the disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, advising that the situation may weigh on growth sentiment.

stories.headline: Pakistan Oil Tanker Makes Rare Entry and Exit Through Hormuz
stories.summary: A Pakistan-flagged tanker that entered the Persian Gulf over the weekend has become the first carrier to exit through the Strait of Hormuz with a crude cargo since a US blockade began on Monday, underscoring just how limited traffic through the vital chokepoint remains.

stories.headline: India’s Rupee Is Asia’s Biggest Gainer on Oil FX Window Report
stories.summary: The Indian rupee led gains in Asia after a report that the central bank has asked state-owned oil refiners to route their dollar purchases through a special credit facility.

stories.headline: Malaysia’s IAQ Group Is Said to Consider 1 Billion Ringgit IPO
stories.summary: IAQ Group is considering an initial public offering in Kuala Lumpur to raise about 1 billion ringgit ($253 million), according to people with knowledge of the matter.

stories.headline: China’s Surging Tech Stock Index Faces Rising Concentration Risk
stories.summary: China’s tech-heavy ChiNext Index is becoming increasingly concentrated as it powers to an 11-year high, buoyed by earnings beats from a small group of heavyweight stocks.

stories.headline: Oaktree’s Senior Credit Executive for Asia Set to Depart Firm
stories.summary: One of Oaktree Capital Group LLC’s most senior investment professionals for Asia is set to leave the firm in the coming months, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: What’s Next for Manycore After Blockbuster HK Debut
stories.summary: Hangzhou-based Manycore Tech, one of the so-called "Six Little Dragons" of Chinese AI startups, surged as much as 187% during its trading debut in Hong Kong. Its co-founder and chairman Victor Huang, who previously worked at Nvidia, says Manycore plans to integrate new technologies and expand into new markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Indian Stocks Risk Deeper Underperformance as Tech-Led North Asia Draws Funds
stories.summary: Market’s appeal among Asia money managers has eroded.

stories.headline: OCBC Is Said to Emerge as Lead Bidder for HSBC Indonesia Assets
stories.summary: Oversea-Chinese Banking Corp. has emerged as the preferred bidder to buy HSBC Holdings Plc’s retail assets in Indonesia, people familiar with the matter said, as the Singaporean lender seeks to boost its presence in Southeast Asia’s largest economy.

stories.headline: Panel Advances Trump’s Triumphal Arch, Even as Key Member Suggests Changes
stories.summary: The Commission of Fine Arts, a Trump-aligned advisory body, granted preliminary approval. But its vice chairman suggested losing statues atop the structure and other revisions before a final vote.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran agrees to hand over ‘nuclear dust’
stories.summary: Iran has not confirmed Trump’s claim. Giving up its highly enriched uranium would be a major step toward an agreement.

stories.headline: House votes to restore protections for Haitians, defying Trump

stories.headline: Armed robbers hold 25 people hostage at Naples bank before fleeing through hole in floor
stories.summary: Thieves believed to have escaped into sewers after holding staff and customers in Crédit Agricole branch for two hoursArmed robbers held 25 people hostage at a bank in Naples for two hours on Thursday, before fleeing through a tunnel.The three thieves entered a branch of Crédit Agricole in the southern Italian city at about 11.30am, taking hostage staff and customers, who were freed by police a couple of hours later. stories.headline: Judge Again Halts Aboveground Construction on Trump Ballroom

stories.headline: Federal judge sets new limits on Trump ballroom construction

stories.headline: Nine killed in second Turkish school shooting in two days
stories.summary: Eight students and one teacher died in the attack, according to Interior Minister Mustafa Cifci.

stories.headline: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues

stories.summary: Péter Magyar, the man who ended Viktor Orbán's 16 years of continuous rule, wants the new parliament to convene in early May.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism

stories.summary: The move has raised questions over parliamentary scrutiny of future rules to deliver planned EU deals.

stories.headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
stories.summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.
